title: PJM Map Document
description: Task-scoped product map: one model, rendered as CAPABILITY (structure) and JOURNEY (behavior) projections.

intent.description: explain: product understanding, canonical journeys. impact: proposed change -> product blast radius + journey regressions (diffs populated). present: journeys destined for screenshot capture + deck export.

task.description: Free-form task statement (bug id, feature request, onboarding question).

session_language.description: ISO code of the human's session language (e.g. 'uk', 'en'). All prose fields MUST be in this language (contract §11). Lint cross-checks: a Cyrillic-script declaration with zero Cyrillic prose is a hard error.

commit_hash.description: HEAD at generation time. Maps are EPHEMERAL: valid for this commit, not maintained afterwards.

analysis_scope.description: Human-readable definition of what is 'in focus'. The perimeter-closure invariant applies within this scope.

scan_coverage.description: Honest declaration of residual uncertainty (unknown unknowns line of defense).

scanned_sources.description: e.g. ['migrations', 'spring annotations', 'rmq bindings config', 'git history 6mo', 'environment manifest']

not_scanned.description: e.g. ['manual prod changes', 'external systems without code in repo']

environment_manifest_ref.description: Path/URL of the PRODUCT.md manifest consumed during generation, if any.

source_root.description: Absolute path to the analyzed repo on the machine running the viewer. When present, the viewer resolves node.source_refs (file:lineStart-lineEnd, repo-relative) to live code: previews the slice in the inspector and offers 'Open in IDE' (code -g). Omit to fall back to embedded code_snippets only.

flow_diffs.description: Proposed BEHAVIORAL changes: how journeys transform under the proposed change. Rendered as ghost/added arrows in JOURNEY view.

removed_step_seqs.description: Steps that disappear under the proposed change.

advisory.description: LLM-written interpretation layer ON TOP of deterministic metrics. Never the other way around.

notes.description: Non-structural observations: UX friction, business risks, operational notes.

open_questions.description: Unroutable black boxes / unconfirmed whys left for the human.

residual_uncertainty.description: Explicit statement of what remains unknown after this analysis.

code_snippets.description: Optional pre-resolved snippets for source_refs (viewer may also read files live).

tours.description: Guided walkthroughs: ordered, map-anchored explanation steps

focus.description: node and/or edge ids highlighted at this step

md.description: one paragraph, rendered as markdown in the viewer side panel

flow_ref.description: optional flow id to auto-play at this step

id.description: Stable semantic id (svc_order). Used in cross-references.

display_id.description: Short human/AI shared coordinate (N2, N2.1, BB1). Rendered semi-transparent in UI; used in chat: 'explain E3', 'investigate BB1'.

kind.description: WHAT it is. Open set; common: capability, screen, role, rule, entity, external_system. NOTE: black box is NOT a kind — it is resolution=suspected.

resolution.description: HOW MUCH we know. suspected = Black Box: cheap broad-scan found a concrete artifact hint, deep-dive not yet done. dismissed nodes are KEPT (with dismissal reason in evidence) so investigations aren't repeated.

scope.description: Relation to meta.analysis_scope, NOT an intrinsic property. peripheral = lives its own life and influences the focused subsystem from the side (trigger, cron, other service via shared DB).

evidence.description: MANDATORY for resolution=suspected|dismissed. Concrete artifact that justified the suspicion (migration line, @Scheduled annotation, rmq binding, git change-coupling stat) or the dismissal reason. A black box without evidence is spam and violates the agent contract.

relevance.description: For suspected nodes: how likely to matter for the current task.

source_refs.description: file:lineStart-lineEnd. Every claim on the diagram must be one click away from real code.

manifest_ref.description: If the node came from the environment manifest rather than code scan.

metrics.description: DETERMINISTIC numbers from code index + git. No LLM involvement — otherwise the heat overlay can't be trusted.

flows_count.description: In how many known flows this node participates.

pos.description: OPTIONAL layout hint. If omitted, the viewer assigns a position via built-in left→right layered auto-layout. Provide pos only to pin a node; mixing pinned and auto nodes is allowed but auto nodes are placed on a grid and may sit near pinned ones.

investigated_at.description: Reserved for future map accumulation. Optional in v0.1.

kind.description: Product-scope relations. suspected_influence: dashed '?' edge from/to a black box. evidenced_by/refuted_by: epistemic edges from an evidence artifact or code node to a hypothesis node — excluded from metrics like suspected_influence.

happy_path: canonical success. failure_path: DESIGNED failure handling — the system behaving as intended under a fault (use for explain-intent failure scenarios). bug_path: reproduction of an ACTUAL defect (debug intent only — implies 'this is broken now'). edge_case: unusual-but-valid input/state. proposed: flow under a proposed diff.

actor.description: Primary actor (role node id) of this journey.

verified_by.description: Honesty marker: was this flow confirmed by an actual run/trace or is it derived/hypothesized?

preface.description: Context arrows before step 1 ('how we got here') so mid-process flows don't float.

explanation.description: What happens and WHY it matters; shown in playback.

screen.description: Screen node id where this step happens (journey integrity: must exist).

business_why.description: Why this step exists for the user/business. Unsourced why must be reflected as suspected via the owning node or advisory.

screenshot.description: Path relative to the map file, or the literal 'pending'.

arrow.description: Atomic behavioral event. MAP derives which edges to light (arrow.edge); SEQUENCE derives activation bars by stack simulation (push on call, pop on return). The call stack is COMPUTED from data, never hand-drawn.

edge.description: Which structural edge this event travels over. Links the two projections.

err.description: return carrying an exception rather than a result.
